reasoning: Authoritative sources tracking AfCFTA ratification progress confirm that Sudan did not ratify the agreement before July 1, 2025. According to data from Tralac (Trade Law Centre) updated as of December 2025, 49 out of 54 signatory countries had formally deposited their instruments of ratification with the African Union Commission. Sudan had "approved ratification" domestically but had not yet deposited its instrument, meaning it had not officially ratified the agreement (and would be the 50th country to do so once deposited). Another source from April 2025 confirming data as of February 2025 also lists Sudan in a table of countries that "have signed but not yet ratified" the AfCFTA agreement. Additionally, Sudan has been engulfed in a severe, wide-scale civil war since April 2023, making the completion of international treaty deposits practically difficult and less of a priority. Because the formal deposit of the instrument of ratification—which constitutes ratification in international law and for the AU—had not occurred by December 2025 (well past the July 2025 deadline), this resolves definitively as a No.
